component.description: 60VIN, 5A, Asynchronous Step-Down Converter with Low Quiescent Current

pin_definitions.description: Bootstrap capacitor connection node to supply the high-side gate driver. Connect a 0.1µF ceramic capacitor between this pin and the SW pin.
Power input. The input voltage range is from 4V to 60V. Connect a suitable input capacitor between this pin and GND.
Enable control pin with internal pull-up current source. Float or provide a logic-high ( ≥ 1.2V) enables the converter; a logic-low forces the device into shutdown mode.
Soft-start and tracking control input. Connect a capacitor from SS to GND to set the soft-start period.
Frequency setting and external synchronous signal input. Connect a resistor from this pin to GND to set the switching frequency.
Output voltage sense. Sense the output voltage at the FB pin through a resistive divider.
Compensation node. Connect external compensation elements to this pin to stabilize the control loop.
Ground. Provide the ground return path for the control circuitry.
Switch node. SW is the switching node that supplies power to the output.
Exposed pad. The exposed pad is internally unconnected and must be soldered to a large PCB copper area.

logical_pins.name: BOOT
logical_pins.description: Bootstrap capacitor connection node to supply the high-side gate driver. Connect a 0.1μF ceramic capacitor between this pin and the SW pin.

logical_pins.name: VIN
logical_pins.description: Power input. The input voltage range is from 4V to 60V. Connect a suitable input capacitor between this pin and GND.

logical_pins.name: EN
logical_pins.description: Enable control pin with internal pull-up current source. Float or provide a logic-high (≥ 1.2V) enables the converter; a logic-low forces the device into shutdown mode.

logical_pins.name: SS/TR
logical_pins.description: Soft-start and tracking control input. Connect a capacitor from SS to GND to set the soft-start period. Do Not leave this pin floating to avoid inrush current during power up.

logical_pins.name: RT/SYNC
logical_pins.description: Frequency setting and external synchronous signal input. Connect a resistor from this pin to GND to set the switching frequency. Tie to a clock source for synchronization to an external frequency.

logical_pins.name: FB
logical_pins.description: Output voltage sense. Sense the output voltage at the FB pin through a resistive divider. The feedback reference voltage is 0.8V typically.

logical_pins.name: COMP
logical_pins.description: Compensation node. Connect external compensation elements to this pin to stabilize the control loop.

logical_pins.name: GND
logical_pins.description: Ground. Provide the ground return path for the control circuitry.

logical_pins.name: SW
logical_pins.description: Switch node. SW is the switching node that supplies power to the output. Connect the output LC filter from SW to the output load.

logical_pins.name: PGOOD
logical_pins.description: Open-drain power-good indication output. Once being started-up, PGOOD will be pulled low to GND if any internal protection is triggered.

logical_pins.name: PAD
logical_pins.description: Exposed pad. The exposed pad is internally unconnected and must be soldered to a large PCB copper area for maximum power dissipation.
